source_jobs.title: Workforce Planner, Sweden
source_jobs.description:
Mission

Neko is redefining what prevention means, from treating illness when it arrives, to sustaining health before it's ever at risk. Our mission: make data-driven, preventative care accessible to more people, before symptoms appear.

 

In a single, non-invasive visit under an hour, proprietary technology and direct clinical care combine to deliver personalised, actionable insights. It's a team that thinks in 10x, not 10%. Every role here plays a part in building a world where prevention is the norm, and where your work genuinely helps people live longer, healthier lives.




ROLE PURPOSE

This role exists to keep Swedish clinics staffed to target coverage. The Workforce Coordinator owns bi-weekly and monthly rota creation, maintains accurate schedules in the workforce management system, and produces the staffing reports that let clinic and market leadership make fast, informed decisions. The role sits within the Operations Planning function and partners directly with clinic leads on coverage, staffing, and labour metrics.




WHAT YOU'LL DELIVER IN THE FIRST 6-12 MONTHS

 - Own accurate, on-time bi-weekly/monthly rota publication for all assigned Swedish clinics, with late changes, swaps, and backfills handled without service disruption.

 - Establish daily tracking of open shifts and coverage gaps across assigned clinics, with escalation to bank pools and agency partners triggered before gaps affect throughput.

 - Administer Quinyx (locations, roles, templates, policies, leave rules) for assigned clinics and configure at least one automation that measurably reduces manual admin time (Tech-enabled, human-centred).

 - Deliver a reliable weekly staffing pack (coverage %, overtime, additional hours, tardiness, utilisation, variance vs plan) that clinic and market leadership use to make staffing decisions.

 - Reduce payroll queries and adjustments through cleaner timesheet reconciliation and consistent HRIS-to-payroll data flow.




MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience in scheduling/rota coordination, clinic operations, or workforce administration.

 - Comfortable in Excel: filters, lookups, pivot basics, conditional formatting; able to analyse CSVs and spot anomalies.

 - Hands-on experience with a WFMS/scheduling system (e.g. Quinyx, Deputy, UKG, Rotageek) or a strong, demonstrable appetite to learn one fast.

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English.

 - Demonstrated ownership of detail-heavy, deadline-driven admin work (the “last mile” that keeps a rota accurate).




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Multi-site healthcare or shift-based operations background.

 - Direct Quinyx experience specifically, rather than an adjacent WFMS.

 - Prior exposure to payroll reconciliation or HRIS-to-payroll data flows.

source_jobs.description:
Mission

Neko is redefining what prevention means, from treating illness when it arrives, to sustaining health before it's ever at risk. Our mission: make data-driven, preventative care accessible to more people, before symptoms appear.

 

In a single, non-invasive visit under an hour, proprietary technology and direct clinical care combine to deliver personalised, actionable insights. It's a team that thinks in 10x, not 10%. Every role here plays a part in building a world where prevention is the norm, and where your work genuinely helps people live longer, healthier lives.





Role Purpose

As UK B2B Lead, you will design and run Neko's first B2B sales strategy & programme, starting in Birmingham and Manchester before expanding to London. This is a build-and-prove role: you will establish our go-to-market approach for corporate clients, run B2B experiments to define the proposition, generate Neko’s first employer deals, and lay the commercial and operational foundations for B2B as a major growth channel alongside our B2C business.



You will work directly with the UK Country Manager and have executive sponsorship from the leadership team. Working alongside the City Marketing Leads, you will ensure B2B and B2C activity are coordinated on the ground in each city.



Your goal as the first B2B hire for Neko is to generate incremental bookings and build the B2B playbook that will be used for Neko’s broader B2B global strategy and new city activation.



What You’ll Deliver in the First 6–12 Months

 - Build a targeted prospect list of companies across Birmingham and Manchester, covering SMB- businesses, hub offices of London-HQ firms, and premium health check buyers

 - Run outreach and activation, focusing on conversion to booking sales while validating key hypotheses that will inform broader B2B proposition

 - Convert at least 2-4 employer deals, with a healthy pipeline of late-stage deals

 - Initiate conversations with key benefit platforms and assess the commercial case for integration

 - Deliver a clear hypothesis readout at Week 8: which employer segments respond, which channels work, whether price holds, and what non-price levers close deals

 - Scale the programme into London, applying learnings from Birmingham and Manchester to a larger and more competitive market

 - Own a growing pipeline of employer accounts and deepen relationships with early clients to secure renewals and case studies

 - Build and refine the B2B city activation playbook - the repeatable framework Neko will use to launch B2B in every new market

 - Work with marketing and product to develop the employer proposition: pricing, packaging, add-ons, and the operational mechanics of group bookings.

 - Partner with the City Marketing Leads to coordinate B2B events and activations that drive awareness, education and conversion with B2B clients (and serve B2C objectives)



Requirements

 - 10+ years of B2B sales or business development experience, with a track record of closing deals and building pipelines from scratch

 - Experience selling into HR, People, or C-suite decision-makers at SMB and enterprise companies - ideally across professional services, tech, or finance

 - Comfortable running a full sales cycle independently: prospecting, outreach, discovery, proposal, and close

 - Existing network in the Birmingham and/or Manchester business community is a strong advantage

 - Commercially sharp - you understand unit economics, can defend pricing, and know when (and when not) to negotiate

 - Organised and data-driven, with the ability to track a pipeline, report on performance, and draw clear learnings from early experiments

 - Comfortable working in an early-stage environment where the playbook does not yet exist - you will be writing it

 - A genuine interest in preventive health and employee wellness; able to speak credibly about the value of proactive health investment to business leaders

source_jobs.title: Electronics Design Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Mission

Neko is redefining what prevention means, from treating illness when it arrives, to sustaining health before it's ever at risk. Our mission: make data-driven, preventative care accessible to more people, before symptoms appear.

 

In a single, non-invasive visit under an hour, proprietary technology and direct clinical care combine to deliver personalised, actionable insights. It's a team that thinks in 10x, not 10%. Every role here plays a part in building a world where prevention is the norm, and where your work genuinely helps people live longer, healthier lives.



Neko Health is a Swedish healthcare technology company co-founded in 2018 by Hjalmar Nilsonne and Daniel Ek. Neko's vision is to shift healthcare from reactive treatment toward preventative health and early detection. This requires completely reimagining the patient's experience and incorporating the latest advances in sensors and AI.

Neko Health has developed a new medical scanning technology concept to make it possible to do broad and non-invasive health data collection that is convenient and affordable for the public. The company is based in Stockholm, offering the Neko Body Scan experience at locations in Stockholm, London and Manchester, with over 500 employees.



About the role

We are looking for a hands-on Electronics Design Engineer to join our Hardware & Firmware organization. In this role, you will design and own electronics for sensor-dense medical systems, working closely with Mechanical, Firmware, Software, Verification & Validation, Manufacturing, and Production teams.You will take responsibility across the full product lifecycle — from early requirements and concept development to verification, validation, and production support — with a strong focus on robustness, data quality, and real-world reliability.This is a practical, lab-facing role for engineers who want to see their designs deployed in real clinical environments. 



What you'll do

 - Own electronics design end-to-end, from requirements and concept through schematic, layout, verification, and production support 

 - Design robust mixed-signal PCBs for sensor-dense medical systems, balancing signal integrity, power, thermal, and EMC constraints 

 - Select and integrate MCUs / DSPs and key components, working closely with firmware and software teams 

 - Lead board bring-up, debugging, and root-cause analysis of prototypes and production hardware 

 - Collaborate cross-functionally to ensure designs are manufacturable, testable, compliant, and scalable 



What you bring

 - Degree in Electrical Engineering (or equivalent practical experience) 

 - Minimum 4 years of hands-on electronics product development experience 

 - Strong experience with mixed-signal PCB design, including schematic, layout, and simulation 

 - Solid understanding of power design, signal integrity, and EMC/EMI in real products 

 - Hands-on experience with board bring-up, debugging, and lab instrumentation in embedded systems 





Bonus points

 - Experience working in the medical or other regulated industries 

 - Experience with RF design or RF-sensitive systems 

 - Experience developing test interfaces or test systems (e.g. Python, LabVIEW, MATLAB)

source_jobs.description:
Mission

Neko is redefining what prevention means, from treating illness when it arrives, to sustaining health before it's ever at risk. Our mission: make data-driven, preventative care accessible to more people, before symptoms appear.

 

In a single, non-invasive visit under an hour, proprietary technology and direct clinical care combine to deliver personalised, actionable insights. It's a team that thinks in 10x, not 10%. Every role here plays a part in building a world where prevention is the norm, and where your work genuinely helps people live longer, healthier lives.



Role Purpose

This is not a traditional laboratory role. We're looking for a Biomedical Scientist who is comfortable working differently, embraces innovation, and takes pride in delivering accurate results at exceptional pace.

Neko Health members come through our clinics for whole-body preventive health screenings, and our laboratories sit at the centre of the patient journey. They are busy, modern spaces, and the service continues to develop as we scale.

This is a hands-on, autonomous role, based on site in one of our central London laboratories. As a Biomedical Scientist, you will operate our Abbott Alinity and Sysmex XN-550 analysers and support the quality systems that allow us to return results within 30 minutes of sample receipt.

If you're a BMS looking to further develop your career in an innovation-led environment, we would love to hear from you.

 

What You'll Deliver in the First 6 - 12 Months

 - Initialise the analysers each day and ready them for sample processing.

 - Perform testing on our Abbott Alinity (biochemistry and immunoassay) and Sysmex XN-550 (haematology) analysers, validating patient results and escalating clinically anomalous or unexpected findings.

 - Carry out daily calibration, internal quality control, and routine maintenance of analysers, troubleshooting and escalating technical issues promptly.

 - Work within our ISO 15189-aligned, UKAS-facing quality management system: author SOP updates, monitor EQA performance, support audits, and investigate non-conformances.

 - Contribute to service improvement work, including analyser verification, reagent evaluation, and software testing.

 - Maintain accurate, auditable records and support performance monitoring across turnaround times, KPIs, and consumables usage.

 - Provide technical guidance to clinical colleagues and support training across the lab team.

 

Minimum Requirements

 - HCPC registration as a Biomedical Scientist, with evidence of ongoing CPD.

 - Minimum one year of post-registration experience in clinical chemistry and immunoassay, including IQC and EQA practice.

 - Demonstrable experience operating an integrated clinical chemistry and immunoassay analyser.

 - Experience working accurately and efficiently under time pressure, suited to a low-volume, fast-turnaround service model.

 

Preferred

 - Post-registration experience in haematology.

 - Hands-on experience operating Sysmex XN-series haematology analysers.

 - Hands-on experience operating Abbott Alinity ci-series.

 - Experience in POCT, rapid diagnostics, or high-throughput clinical settings.

 - Experience with laboratory information management systems (LIMS), digital result reporting, or involvement in LIMS development and user acceptance testing.

source_jobs.title: Dermatologist
source_jobs.description:
Mission

Neko is redefining what prevention means, from treating illness when it arrives, to sustaining health before it's ever at risk. Our mission: make data-driven, preventative care accessible to more people, before symptoms appear.

 

In a single, non-invasive visit under an hour, proprietary technology and direct clinical care combine to deliver personalised, actionable insights. It's a team that thinks in 10x, not 10%. Every role here plays a part in building a world where prevention is the norm, and where your work genuinely helps people live longer, healthier lives.



Role Purpose

This is a clinical role at the center of Neko's dermatology pathway. Every member who receives an abnormal skin finding from their Neko scan will be seen, assessed, and triaged by this clinician — determining whether findings can be reassured and discharged or require referral to a community dermatologist.

You will not be managing ongoing dermatology care or initiating treatment for complex skin conditions — those cases go directly to a community dermatologist. You will be the clinical authority on whether a finding is actionable, and the person responsible for ensuring the right members reach the right specialist without delay.

What You'll Deliver in the First 6–12 Months

 - All abnormal skin scan findings are reviewed, interpreted, and triaged with accuracy and speed — no member falls through the gap.

 - Clear, structured clinical documentation is in place for every encounter, fully compliant with applicable state and federal requirements.

 - Dermatology triage protocols are actively refined in collaboration with the Medical Innovation team, keeping pace with AAD guidelines and evolving US regulatory standards.

 - A community referral network is established and maintained — dermatologists and dermatopathology services — across each market we operate in.

Responsibilities

Clinical — Dermatology Triage and Follow-Up

 - Review all abnormal skin findings from Neko scans, including pigmented lesions, suspicious moles, and common skin cancers.

 - Interpret findings and triage as: reassure and discharge, monitor at next annual scan, or refer to community dermatology.

 - Maintain accurate, structured clinical documentation for every encounter in compliance with applicable state and federal requirements.

Governance and Quality

 - Ensure dermatology triage decisions are consistent with Neko's clinical protocols, current AAD guidelines, and applicable US regulatory standards.

 - Contribute to the development and ongoing refinement of US dermatology triage protocols, working with the Medical Innovation team.

Referral Network Development

 - Build and maintain relationships with community dermatologists and dermatopathology services across markets.

Minimum Requirements

 - MD or DO degree with completion of a dermatology residency from an ACGME-accredited program.

 - Board certification (or eligibility) in Dermatology (ABD).

 - Current licensure to practice medicine in New York State, in good standing with the NYSED.

 - Demonstrated ability to independently assess, triage, and manage skin lesions including pigmented lesions, suspicious moles, and common skin cancers.

 - Proficiency with dermoscopy, or completion of an accredited dermoscopy training program prior to start.

 - Familiarity with AAD guidelines and their application to a preventive health population.

Preferred

 - Dermoscopy certification (SDC, IDC, or equivalent).

 - Experience in a high-volume outpatient dermatology or preventive health setting.

 - Telehealth clinical experience.

 - Experience with AI-assisted diagnostic or imaging interpretation platforms.

 - Interest in or experience with health tech, preventive medicine, or direct-to-consumer healthcare models.

 - Multilingual — Spanish particularly advantageous for the NYC market.

 - Multiple state medical licenses.

source_jobs.description:
Mission

Neko is redefining what prevention means, from treating illness when it arrives, to sustaining health before it's ever at risk. Our mission: make data-driven, preventative care accessible to more people, before symptoms appear.

 

In a single, non-invasive visit under an hour, proprietary technology and direct clinical care combine to deliver personalised, actionable insights. It's a team that thinks in 10x, not 10%. Every role here plays a part in building a world where prevention is the norm, and where your work genuinely helps people live longer, healthier lives.




ROLE PURPOSE

As a Data Engineer within our Data-to-AI platform, you will play a critical role in enabling healthcare innovation through robust, scalable, and secure data systems. You will design and build core data platform components, advanced data models, and ingestion frameworks that power analytics, machine learning, and data-driven decision making across the organization. Working with sensitive healthcare data, you will ensure strong governance, data quality, and regulatory compliance remain foundational to the platform.




WHAT YOU’LL DELIVER IN THE FIRST 6–12 MONTHS

• Build and productionize scalable batch and streaming data pipelines supporting platform and research workloads.

• Design and implement robust data models across databases and lakehouse systems to support analytics and machine learning.

• Establish strong data quality, monitoring, and lineage practices improving reliability, traceability, and trust in platform data.

• Contribute to scalable and well-governed data platform infrastructure supporting performance, compliance, and growth.

• Partner closely with data scientists, analysts, and engineering teams to enable reliable data contracts and high-quality datasets.




RESPONSIBILITIES

• Build and own scalable data pipelines for ingestion, integration, and processing across batch and streaming systems.

• Architect and maintain robust data models across databases and lakehouse platforms supporting analytics and ML workloads.

• Develop and own core data platform components and infrastructure.

• Ensure data integrity and quality through monitoring, alerting, lineage, and traceability.

• Manage and optimize data infrastructure including clusters, storage, and compute resources.

• Implement metrics and observability across services using logging, tracing, and monitoring.

• Troubleshoot production issues, pipeline failures, and performance bottlenecks.

• Collaborate cross-functionally with data scientists, analysts, and backend engineers on modelling, governance, and integration.




MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS

• Strong programming skills in Python and SQL.

• Hands-on experience with relational and NoSQL databases, data lakes, and lakehouse technologies (e.g. Delta Lake, Parquet).

• Experience working with cloud infrastructure (Azure, AWS, or GCP).

• Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and automated testing practices.

• Strong data quality and observability experience including metrics, tracing, and alerting.

• Experience working with data governance practices including lineage, cataloguing, access control, data contracts, and regulatory compliance.




ABOUT THE ENGINEERING TEAM


DISTRIBUTED AND REMOTE FIRST

Neko Health has nearly 100 full-time engineers working across Berlin, Chamonix, Hamburg, Lisbon, Marseille, Vilnius, and Stockholm, spanning disciplines such as Hardware Engineering, Firmware Development, Electrical Design, Algorithm Development, Machine Learning, Optronics Research, and Software Engineering.

Our technology stack includes React, TypeScript, C++, Python, and C# with ASP.NET http://ASP.NET Core. We use Azure Cosmos DB and Azure Active Directory for authentication.

source_jobs.title: Medical Assistant
source_jobs.description:
Mission

Neko is redefining what prevention means, from treating illness when it arrives, to sustaining health before it's ever at risk. Our mission: make data-driven, preventative care accessible to more people, before symptoms appear.

 

In a single, non-invasive visit under an hour, proprietary technology and direct clinical care combine to deliver personalised, actionable insights. It's a team that thinks in 10x, not 10%. Every role here plays a part in building a world where prevention is the norm, and where your work genuinely helps people live longer, healthier lives.



Role Purpose:

You're the person the clinic can rely on when it's busy — not because of seniority, but because of how you show up. Calm when the pace picks up. Consistent when things get hard. The kind of person colleagues quietly lean on, and members remember.

As an Operator at Neko, you'll be the welcoming face of our clinic and a key contributor to our preventive care model. You'll guide members through their visit, perform high-quality clinical assessments, and ensure every interaction reflects genuine care — not just an efficient process.



What You'll Deliver in the First 6–12 Months:

 - Welcome, assist, and guide members throughout their clinic visit, ensuring every interaction reflects a member-first mindset.

 - Perform accurate, high-quality clinical assessments — vital signs, grip strength, eye pressure, ECGs, point-of-care tests, and phlebotomy — following established standards and protocols.

 - Maintain exam rooms and uphold infection-control standards to ensure a safe, clean, and efficient clinical environment.

 - Document assessments accurately and update member records with precision, supporting continuity of care across the clinical team.

 - Work closely with fellow Operators and clinical staff to refine workflows, elevate quality, and support clinic operations as Neko scales.



What We're Hiring For:

 - Genuine Warmth -- You take the member experience personally. People leave your care feeling seen, not processed — and you notice the difference. The warmth is visible before you've said anything. Members feel it in the way you explain something, the way you check in, the way you say goodbye.

 - Reliability Under Pressure - You're calm and consistent when the pace picks up. You manage multiple members and competing tasks without losing quality or warmth. When things break down, you're matter-of-fact — not dramatic, not dismissive. Steady.

 - Natural Team Anchor -- You're the kind of person others naturally lean on — and you take pride in being that person. You don't wait to be asked. You step in, share what you know, and hold the standard. Not because it's in your job description, but because that's how you work.

 - Fluent With the Tools -- Our technology is what makes the visit possible — and you're comfortable with it. You pick up new systems quickly and stay composed when something doesn't work the way it should, keeping the member in front of you at ease while you sort it out. When a tool isn't serving the experience, you say so. That's how we make it better for the next member.



Minimum Requirements

 - 1+ year of clinical experience in a patient-facing role

Role Purpose

This is not a traditional laboratory role. We're looking for a Biomedical Scientist who is comfortable working differently, embraces innovation, and takes pride in delivering accurate results at exceptional pace.

Neko Health members come through our clinics for whole-body preventive health screenings, and our laboratories sit at the centre of the patient journey. They are busy, modern spaces, and the service continues to develop as we scale.

This is a hands-on, autonomous role, based on site in one of our central London laboratories. As a Biomedical Scientist, you will operate our Abbott Alinity and Sysmex XN-550 analysers and support the quality systems that allow us to return results within 30 minutes of sample receipt.

If you're a BMS looking to further develop your career in an innovation-led environment, we would love to hear from you.

 

What You'll Deliver in the First 6 - 12 Months

 - Initialise the analysers each day and ready them for sample processing.

 - Perform testing on our Abbott Alinity (biochemistry and immunoassay) and Sysmex XN-550 (haematology) analysers, validating patient results and escalating clinically anomalous or unexpected findings.

 - Carry out daily calibration, internal quality control, and routine maintenance of analysers, troubleshooting and escalating technical issues promptly.

 - Work within our ISO 15189-aligned, UKAS-facing quality management system: author SOP updates, monitor EQA performance, support audits, and investigate non-conformances.

 - Contribute to service improvement work, including analyser verification, reagent evaluation, and software testing.

 - Maintain accurate, auditable records and support performance monitoring across turnaround times, KPIs, and consumables usage.

 - Provide technical guidance to clinical colleagues and support training across the lab team.

 

Minimum Requirements

 - HCPC registration as a Biomedical Scientist, with evidence of ongoing CPD.

 - Minimum one year of post-registration experience in clinical chemistry and immunoassay, including IQC and EQA practice.

 - Demonstrable experience operating an integrated clinical chemistry and immunoassay analyser.

 - Experience working accurately and efficiently under time pressure, suited to a low-volume, fast-turnaround service model.

 

Preferred

 - Post-registration experience in haematology.

 - Hands-on experience operating Sysmex XN-series haematology analysers.

 - Hands-on experience operating Abbott Alinity ci-series.

 - Experience in POCT, rapid diagnostics, or high-throughput clinical settings.

 - Experience with laboratory information management systems (LIMS), digital result reporting, or involvement in LIMS development and user acceptance testing.

 - Track and act on clinic metrics — rebooking, CSAT, NPS, utilization, throughput — and partner with medical leadership to keep raising the quality of care across the clinic.

 - Own incident reporting, risk management, and member-concern resolution in partnership with Customer Care, and keep the clinic compliant with HIPAA, OSHA, and internal SOPs.

What We're Hiring For:

 - You Build the Team, Not Just Manage -- It You don't just run 1:1s — your people get better because of how you lead them. Physicians sharpen their practice under your mentorship. Minimum Requirements:

 - 5+ years of leadership experience in a clinical setting, including direct management of physicians or licensed clinicians

 - Experience in a high-growth, startup, or early-stage healthcare environment preferred

 - Active Basic Life Support (BLS) certification.

As Senior Biomedical Scientist, you hold senior professional responsibility for the performance of our laboratory service, ensuring every patient result is accurate, clinically appropriate, and delivered within 30 minutes.

This is an autonomous role with end-to-end ownership of our biochemistry and haematology service across two of our clinic-based laboratories. You will combine deep technical and clinical expertise, quality leadership, and remote supervisory responsibility.

The scope of this role is growing fast. You won't just be running our existing labs - you'll help us scale, setting up new laboratories across London and other cities in the UK.

This is a hybrid role, with one day a week on-site at one of our London clinics and the remainder worked remotely. You will also take part in an on-call rota, together with the wider team.

You hold the standard without making it feel like pressure.

As Lead Operator at Neko, you’ll own the day-to-day performance of the operator team and the overall quality of scanning operations and member experience. You’ll be the primary interface between HQ and the clinic floor — translating priorities into consistent, high-quality delivery while remaining hands-on as a scanning operator yourself.

Clinic: Park Modern

Hours: Full-time

Application Deadline: Friday 5th June




WHAT YOU'LL DO:
